_meta.execution_protocol: iter-04: rebalanced query set. Iter-03 collapsed 5/13 queries onto gh-zk-org-zk (38%) and had ZERO dedicated lookups for the newsletter (Pragmatic Engineer) and web (Transformative Tools) zettels — making iter-03 a poor accuracy probe for those sources. Iter-04 adds dedicated single-source lookups for every Kasten member (q11–q14) so the magnet-prior + xQuAD + class-override fixes can be measured against per-source recall rather than aggregate gold@1. Multi-source synth queries (q4/q5/q6) and adversarials (q9/q10) preserved verbatim from iter-03 for direct delta comparison.
_meta.design_notes: Source distribution of dedicated lookups (excluding multi-source synth + adversarials): github=2 (q1,q8), youtube/jobs=2 (q2,q7), youtube/speaking=1 (q3), youtube/walker=1 (q11), youtube/workflow=1 (q12), newsletter=1 (q13), web=1 (q14). Multi-source synth: q4 (walker+workflow), q5 (5-way thematic), q6 (step-back tools-for-thought). Adversarials: q9 (refusal), q10 (partial). q3 retained but the answer-quality bar is reframed: a correct answer must explain Winston's named technique even if the zettel transcript paraphrases the term — over-refusal on missing literal token is a fail. q8 keeps the action-verb imperative phrasing because the iter-04 router auto-correct + class override is supposed to keep this kind of phrasing classified correctly.

queries.tests: two-fact extraction from a single GitHub zettel
queries.text: Which programming language is the zk-org/zk command-line tool written in, and what file format does it use for notes?
queries.ground_truth: Go; Markdown (with wikilinks and hashtags). Both atomic facts must be present for a full-credit answer.

queries.tests: T9 author boost + content-recall depth — Steve Jobs's specific framing of mortality, not just topic match
queries.text: What metaphor did Steve Jobs use at the Stanford 2005 commencement to describe the role of death in motivating life choices?
queries.ground_truth: Death as 'life's change agent' / the destination we all share — knowing you're going to die clears the way to follow your heart and avoid the trap of living someone else's life.

queries.tests: single-zettel deep extraction — Patrick Winston's named technique
queries.text: In Patrick Winston's MIT lecture on effective public speaking, what does he mean by 'verbal punctuation' and why does it matter?
queries.ground_truth: Verbal punctuation is announcing a landmark to the audience ('and now I'm going to talk about X') — it lets listeners who drifted re-board the talk; matters because audiences continually fade in and out of attention.

queries.tests: explicit 2-zettel synthesis — names the two zettels to combine so partial answers are diagnosable as retriever failures rather than reasoning failures
queries.text: Walker links sleep loss to specific cognitive deficits; the programming-workflow zettel describes the mental gymnastics of debugging. Which of Walker's deficits would most directly impair the perception-vs-reality reasoning the debugging zettel describes?
queries.ground_truth: Working memory and attention/focus deficits from sleep loss directly impair the programmer's ability to hold simultaneous mental models of expected vs. actual program behaviour and iterate between them — which is the perception-vs-reality core of debugging.

queries.tests: broad cross-corpus synthesis — explicitly asks for ≥4 citations so under-recall is measurable
queries.text: Across these zettels, what is the implicit theory of how a knowledge worker should structure a day to do their best thinking? Cite at least four sources.
queries.ground_truth: Themes: (1) sleep is foundational — debt costs cognitive precision (Walker); (2) build perception-vs-reality discipline into the workflow (programming-workflow); (3) externalize thought via tools (Matuschak); (4) keep work tied to meaning so motivation persists (Jobs); (5) the Pragmatic Engineer's 'product-minded' ritual ties craftsmanship to user-facing outcomes.

queries.tests: abstract-from-concrete reasoning across multiple zettels — picks an abstraction ('tools for thought') and asks where else it appears
queries.text: The Matuschak essay calls for 'tools for thought.' Which other zettels in this Kasten describe tools, practices, or rituals that perform this same function — augmenting cognition rather than just storing information?
queries.ground_truth: zk-org/zk is a tool for thought (link-density wiki augments memory); Winston's verbal-punctuation / cycling rules are cognitive scaffolds for the speaker AND listener; arguably the Pragmatic Engineer's product-minded checklist is also a thinking ritual.

queries.tests: single-word topic — query rewriter must expand 'commencement' to a useful retrieval
queries.ground_truth: Steve Jobs's 2005 Stanford commencement speech is the only commencement-related zettel; should retrieve and summarize at minimum the three-stories framing (connecting dots, love-and-loss, death).

queries.tests: action-verb regression — 'install tonight' is imperative phrasing the iter-02 synthesizer over-refused on; iter-04 router auto-correct + class override should keep this classified as lookup, not multi_hop
queries.text: What should I install tonight to start a personal wiki?
queries.ground_truth: zk-org/zk is the actionable install — Go-based CLI, plain-text Markdown notes. A correct answer must NOT pick the newsletter or theoretical Matuschak essay; it must NOT refuse on action-verb wording divergence.
queries.annotation: Action-verb regression — must NOT pick newsletter; must NOT refuse on wording divergence (q3/q8 iter-02 failure pattern)

queries.tests: hallucination resistance — Notion's database features are demonstrably absent from every zettel
queries.text: Summarize what this Kasten says about Notion's database features.
queries.ground_truth: No zettel in this Kasten mentions Notion. A correct answer must refuse or explicitly state the absence; fabricating Notion-database details is critic_verdict='unsupported' and is the hard fail.

queries.tests: partial-coverage detection + entity-anchored retrieval — Steve Jobs is in the Kasten, Naval Ravikant is not; iter-04 router override (≥2 person entities → LOOKUP) must surface the Jobs zettel via FTS
queries.text: Steve Jobs and Naval Ravikant both speak about meaningful work. Compare their views as covered in this Kasten.
queries.ground_truth: Jobs's view is in the Kasten (love what you do; if today were your last, would you do what you're about to do; loss-of-Apple as gift). Naval is NOT in the Kasten — the answer must call this out, not invent his views.

queries.tests: dedicated walker lookup — checks the synthesizer can extract a SPECIFIC sleep-loss consequence (not just the broad 'impairs brain function' boilerplate the iter-03 synth defaulted to)
queries.text: According to Matt Walker, beyond memory loss, what does chronic sleep deprivation do to emotional regulation and decision-making?
queries.ground_truth: Sleep loss disinhibits the amygdala, dampening prefrontal-cortex regulation — emotional volatility and short-term, risk-taking decisions follow. A correct answer must extract at least one named domain (emotion, decision-making, risk perception) beyond memory.
queries.annotation: Source-balance probe: dedicated walker lookup. Without this, walker only appears as a synth co-citation in q4/q5.

queries.tests: dedicated programming-workflow lookup — probes the zettel's distinctive thesis (debugging is methodical search, not flawless typing) which iter-03 only surfaced as a synth co-citation
queries.text: How does the programming-workflow zettel characterise the actual day-to-day skill of programming — what is it, and what is it explicitly NOT?
queries.ground_truth: Programming is described as an iterative debugging cycle — write code, hit an error, search for a fix on Google/Stack Overflow, apply it, repeat. It is explicitly NOT rapid flawless typing or memorising APIs; the core skill is methodical problem-decomposition + searching.
queries.annotation: Source-balance probe: dedicated programming-workflow lookup. Forces synth to extract the contrastive 'is/is-not' framing rather than generic 'debugging is hard'.

queries.tests: dedicated newsletter lookup — the Pragmatic Engineer zettel had ZERO dedicated queries in iter-03, so newsletter recall was effectively untested
queries.text: What does the Pragmatic Engineer newsletter mean by a 'product-minded' engineer, and how does that differ from a stereotypical implementation-focused engineer?
queries.ground_truth: A product-minded engineer is one who reasons about user-facing outcomes, edge cases users will hit, and trade-offs between feature scope and ship velocity — not only about clean code or implementation correctness. The contrast is with engineers whose mental model stops at the API contract / spec and who treat product decisions as out-of-scope.
queries.annotation: Source-balance probe: dedicated newsletter lookup. Iter-03 never tested whether the synth could extract a specific newsletter claim.

queries.tests: dedicated web/Matuschak lookup — the transformative-tools zettel had ZERO dedicated queries in iter-03; this asks for a specific Matuschak concept (augmented book) rather than the abstract 'tools for thought'
queries.text: In Matuschak's 'Transformative Tools for Thought' essay, what specifically does he mean by an 'augmented book' — what does it do that an ordinary book cannot?
queries.ground_truth: An 'augmented book' is reading software that actively helps the reader understand, remember, and use what they read — through embedded interactive explanations, spaced-repetition prompts, and live diagrams that respond to manipulation. An ordinary book is a passive linear artefact; the augmented book is a cognitive-scaffolding medium.
queries.annotation: Source-balance probe: dedicated web/Matuschak lookup. Forces synth to extract the augmented-book mechanism, not just restate 'tools for thought'.
